topic: Someone discovers their recipe has become famous under another name
raw_output:
[story 1]

Eleanor stared at the glossy magazine spread, the headline screaming the name ‘The Golden Whisk’ next to a picture of a pastry that, undeniably, looked exactly like hers. Her hands, usually steady when kneading dough, trembled slightly as she traced the elegant script. It was a bitter taste rising in her throat, sharp and metallic. She remembered the late nights, the perfectly balanced ratios, the secret ingredient she’d perfected over decades, all now belonging to some stranger. A slow, cold tightening began in her chest, settling deep beneath her ribs. She picked up her own worn, stained recipe card, the one tucked away in the back of her cookbook, and a cruel smile touched her lips. She needed to see how far this imitation could go before she decided what to do next.

[story 2]

"They call it the 'Midnight Truffle,'" the interviewer had cooed, eyes shining with manufactured awe. Liam listened from the corner of the room, his jaw clenching so tight a muscle jumped visibly in his cheek. He watched the glowing reviews scroll across his phone screen, each endorsement a fresh sting. He felt a strange, hot pressure building behind his eyes, a need to shatter something fragile. He walked over to the counter, his movements sharp and deliberate. He didn't look at the chef who was currently basking in the attention; he looked only at the ingredients, his gaze lingering on the precise measurements he’d scribbled down years ago. He picked up a bottle of vanilla extract, his knuckles white, and set it down with a deliberate, echoing clink.

[story 3]

Sarah meticulously rearranged the pantry, every jar and spice bottle aligned with surgical precision. The recognition hit her like a physical blow—the signature swirl of her signature buttercream, the exact fermentation time she’d perfected. It wasn't just a coincidence; it was a theft, a blatant appropriation of her life’s work. A low growl escaped her throat, a sound barely audible over the gentle hum of the refrigerator. She began compiling a list, not of compliments, but of weaknesses, of minor errors in the public presentation of the successful recipe. Her fingers moved quickly across the keyboard, drafting a series of pointed, perfectly worded critiques, each sentence designed to expose the hollowness beneath the polished facade.
